whats_new.title: Jakarta EE 9.1 full platform support
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 15.1.1.0.0 is a fully compatible implementation of Jakarta EE 9.1.

whats_new.title: Java SE 17 and 21 certification
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 15c provides Java SE 17 and Java SE 21 certification.

whats_new.title: OpenRewrite upgrade recipes
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server application upgrade tooling provides OpenRewrite recipes for Jakarta namespace, descriptor, Java, Maven, and Spring migration tasks.

whats_new.title: Certificate Management Service technical preview
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 15c introduces a Certificate Management Service technical preview for obtaining and storing certificates in domain keystores.

whats_new.title: Separate XA recovery database credentials
whats_new.description: XA data sources can use separate database credentials for XA recovery processing and application processing.

behavior_changes.title: Jakarta namespace migration
behavior_changes.description: Applications must move from Java EE javax namespaces to Jakarta EE jakarta namespaces for the WebLogic Server 15.1.1.0.0 application environment.

behavior_changes.title: Applications must be modified and recompiled
behavior_changes.description: Oracle documents that applications from WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4.0, 14.1.1.0.0, and 14.1.2.0.0 must be modified and recompiled for WebLogic Server 15.1.1.0.0.

behavior_changes.title: Jakarta EE namespace changes from javax to jakarta
behavior_changes.description: The primary Jakarta EE 9.1 platform change is the package namespace move from javax.* to jakarta.* APIs.

behavior_changes.title: Technical preview certificate service is not production-ready
behavior_changes.description: Oracle marks the Certificate Management Service as a technical preview for testing only, not for production environments.

deprecated.title: Legacy Jakarta EE specifications removed
deprecated.description: Jakarta EE 9 removes legacy specifications such as JAXR, XML RPC, Deployment, Management, and Distributed Interoperability.

deprecated.title: javax namespace no longer matches the target platform
deprecated.description: Applications using Java EE javax.* APIs must be migrated to Jakarta EE jakarta.* APIs for WebLogic Server 15.1.1.0.0.

deprecated.title: Legacy XML RPC specification removed from Jakarta EE 9
deprecated.description: XML RPC 1.1 is among the legacy specifications removed from the Jakarta EE 9 platform supported by WebLogic Server 15c.

deprecated.title: Legacy JAXR specification removed from Jakarta EE 9
deprecated.description: Jakarta XML Registries, formerly Java API for XML Registries, is among the specifications removed from Jakarta EE 9.

whats_new.title: Jakarta EE 8 full platform support
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 provides Jakarta EE 8 full platform support.

whats_new.title: Java SE 17 and 21 certification
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 is certified for Java SE 17 and Java SE 21.

whats_new.title: WebLogic Migration Analysis Tool
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 adds a command line migration analysis utility that identifies removed or unused APIs in WebLogic applications.

whats_new.title: OpenRewrite recipes
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 provides OpenRewrite recipes to simplify migrating WebLogic applications to a new WebLogic and Java version.

whats_new.title: Secured production mode enabled by default
whats_new.description: When production mode is selected in WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0, secured production mode defaults are automatically enabled.

whats_new.title: OpenID Connect authentication provider
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 adds a WebLogic OpenID Connect provider for authentication and identity assertion.

whats_new.title: Cluster-targeted JMS automatic rebalance
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 adds an option to automatically rebalance running cluster-targeted JMS instances when the system is idle.

behavior_changes.title: 14c JDK interoperability limitations
behavior_changes.description: Oracle documents interoperability limitations for WebLogic Server 14.1.2.0.0 instances and Java IIOP clients running on JDK 17 or 21.

behavior_changes.title: Production mode enables stricter security defaults
behavior_changes.description: Selecting production mode automatically enables secured production mode, changing default security behavior for new domains.

behavior_changes.title: Demo certificate authority is per-domain
behavior_changes.description: The demonstration certificate authority is unique to each new domain and uses PKCS12 identity and trust keystores.

deprecated.title: Legacy API migration analysis required
deprecated.description: Oracle recommends using migration analysis tooling to identify APIs removed or no longer used in WebLogic Server 14c.

whats_new.title: Java EE 7 application platform
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 12c Release 2 provides a Java EE 7 application server line for enterprise Java applications.

whats_new.title: Runtime improvements
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4 includes runtime improvements for deployment, JDBC data sources, JTA, messaging, monitoring WebSocket applications, RESTful web services, and dynamic clusters.

whats_new.title: WebLogic Kubernetes Toolkit integration
whats_new.description: Oracle documents Kubernetes tooling for migration, domain management, deployment, monitoring, and image patching of WebLogic Server environments.

whats_new.title: WebLogic Server slim installer
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4 adds a lightweight slim installer that can be run from the command line.

whats_new.title: SNMPv3 default protocol
whats_new.description: When SNMP is enabled in WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4.0 and later, SNMPv3 is enabled by default.

behavior_changes.title: Application name behavior change
behavior_changes.description: WebLogic Server 12.2.1 includes deployment behavior changes, including behavior for application names.

behavior_changes.title: SNMPv3 replaces older SNMP defaults
behavior_changes.description: The default SNMP protocol changes to SNMPv3 when SNMP is enabled, because SNMPv1 and SNMPv2 use clear text passwords.

behavior_changes.title: Latest REST management resource version changes
behavior_changes.description: In WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4.0, the latest REST management resource version becomes 12.2.1.4.0 while older REST resource versions are deprecated.

deprecated.title: Multitenant domain partitions deprecated
deprecated.description: WebLogic Server Multitenant domain partitions, resource groups, resource group templates, and virtual targets are deprecated in WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4.0.

deprecated.title: JAXR removed
deprecated.description: The Java API for XML Registries is removed from Oracle WebLogic Server as of version 12.2.1.

deprecated.title: SNMP v1 and v2 deprecated
deprecated.description: WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4.0 deprecates SNMPv1 and SNMPv2 configuration in favor of SNMPv3.

deprecated.title: WebLogic jCOM deprecated
deprecated.description: WebLogic jCOM is deprecated as of WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4.0.

deprecated.title: OPatchAutoFMW deprecated
deprecated.description: OPatchAutoFMW is deprecated as of WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4.0.

deprecated.title: WebLogic full client deprecated
deprecated.description: The WebLogic full client wlfullclient.jar is deprecated; Oracle recommends T3 client or Install client alternatives.

deprecated.title: Log4j integration deprecated
deprecated.description: Using Log4j with the WebLogic logging service is deprecated; Log4j 2 and later are not supported in WebLogic Server.

whats_new.title: Java SE 7 support
whats_new.description: WebLogic Server 10.3.6 adds Java SE 7 support for supported configurations.

whats_new.title: Updated WebLogic Server 11g feature set
whats_new.description: Oracle documents new and changed functionality for configuration, deployment, JDBC, JMS, JTA, security, Spring, TopLink, web applications, and web services in WebLogic Server 10.3.6.
